title: WASM target — core runtime compilation to WebAssembly
purpose: Rust compiles natively to WebAssembly. Compiling beamr to WASM enables running BEAM processes in browsers and edge workers. This brief ensures the core runtime (term representation, heap, interpreter) compiles to wasm32-unknown-unknown without std dependencies.
task: Make the beamr core crate compile to wasm32-unknown-unknown target.

requirements.title: no_std compatibility audit
requirements.spec: Identify and gate std-dependent code behind feature flags.
acceptance: All std::thread usage gated behind a 'threads' feature (enabled by default)
All std::net usage gated behind a 'net' feature
All std::fs usage gated behind a 'fs' feature
Core term, heap, interpreter code compiles with no_std + alloc
Test: cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p beamr --no-default-features passes

requirements.title: Single-threaded scheduler for WASM
requirements.spec: A scheduler variant that runs on a single thread for WASM targets.
acceptance: WasmScheduler runs the event loop cooperatively (no OS threads)
Processes yield via reduction counting (same as normal)
Host (JavaScript) calls run_until_idle in requestAnimationFrame or similar
Test: WasmScheduler executes a simple function to completion

requirements.title: WASM host bindings
requirements.spec: Define the FFI surface for JavaScript interop.
acceptance: #[wasm_bindgen] exports: create_vm(), load_module(bytes), spawn(m, f, a), run_step()
JavaScript can create a VM, load .beam modules, and step execution
Results are returned as JSON-serialized values
Test: wasm-pack test passes with a simple Gleam function

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement multi-threading in WASM — SharedArrayBuffer support is a future optimization
SHALL NOT implement file I/O in WASM — NIF stubs return errors for unavailable operations
SHALL NOT implement distribution in WASM — single-node only
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
